_meta.name: p30
_meta.method: Central saturated-core centroid, edge-supported side parhelia, and visual confirmation against the full 22-degree halo diagnostic overlay.
_meta.status: belt-y FF anchor with parhelion-y tilt / edge-support flag; do not treat as a new inversion-route verdict
_meta.note: The 22-degree halo is strong, but both parhelia are near image edges and the side brightness is vertically broad. Use this as a belt-y FF anchor, with tilt/edge uncertainty recorded.

r22_source_note: Per existing _meta: 'The 22-degree halo is strong.' R22 is from a visible 22° halo ring fit, independent of the parhelion. Pass B1 classification 2026-05-13; lever sec(h)-1 = 1.90%, just below the 2% threshold — anchor-noise-bounded, clean residual is informational, not route-validating. Eligible with low-lever caveat.

parhelion.tilt_flag: left/right y differ by 19 px; side features are edge-supported and vertically broad
